# ado-sync

Bidirectional sync CLI between local test specs and Azure DevOps Test Cases.
npm package: `ado-sync` | bin: `ado-sync` and `ado-sync-mcp`

## What it does

- `push` — reads local test files, creates new Azure DevOps Test Cases (and writes IDs back into the files), or updates existing ones when content changes
- `pull` — reads Test Cases from Azure DevOps and updates matching local files
- `status` — dry-run diff: shows what would change without touching anything
- `diff` — field-level diff showing exactly which fields (title, steps, description) differ
- `publish-test-results` — publishes TRX / JUnit XML / NUnit XML / Cucumber JSON / Playwright JSON / CTRF JSON results to an Azure DevOps Test Run
- `generate` — creates local spec files (.feature or .md) from Azure DevOps User Stories
- `validate` — checks config validity and Azure connectivity
- `init` — interactive wizard that generates ado-sync.json

## Install

```
npm install -g ado-sync
```

## Minimum config (ado-sync.json)

```json
{
  "orgUrl": "https://dev.azure.com/YOUR-ORG",
  "project": "YOUR-PROJECT",
  "auth": { "type": "pat", "token": "$AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN" },
  "testPlan": { "id": 12345 },
  "local": { "type": "TYPE", "include": "GLOB" }
}
```

Required fields: `orgUrl`, `project`, `auth`, `testPlan.id`, `local.type`, `local.include`

## Framework → local.type mapping

| Files present in repo | Use local.type | include glob |
|---|---|---|
| `*.feature` (Cucumber/Gherkin) | `gherkin` | `specs/**/*.feature` |
| `*.feature` (SpecFlow / C#) | `gherkin` | `Features/**/*.feature` |
| `*.md` prose specs | `markdown` | `specs/**/*.md` |
| `*.cs` (MSTest / NUnit) | `csharp` | `**/Tests/**/*.cs` |
| `*.java` (JUnit 4/5 / TestNG) | `java` | `**/src/test/**/*.java` |
| `*.py` (pytest) | `python` | `tests/**/*.py` |
| `*.spec.ts` / `*.spec.js` (Playwright) | `playwright` | `tests/**/*.spec.ts` |
| `*.cy.ts` / `*.cy.js` (Cypress) | `cypress` | `cypress/e2e/**/*.cy.ts` |
| `*.test.ts` / `*.test.js` (Jest) | `javascript` | `src/**/*.test.ts` |
| `*.js` / `*.ts` (Jasmine / WebdriverIO) | `javascript` | `test/**/*.spec.js` |
| `*.js` / `*.ts` (Detox) | `detox` | `e2e/**/*.test.ts` |
| `*.java` / `*.kt` (Espresso) | `espresso` | `app/src/androidTest/**/*.java` |
| `*.swift` (XCUITest) | `xcuitest` | `UITests/**/*.swift` |
| `*_test.dart` (Flutter) | `flutter` | `test/**/*_test.dart` |
| `*.robot` (Robot Framework) | `robot` | `tests/**/*.robot` |
| `*.csv` (ADO tabular export) | `csv` | `testcases/**/*.csv` |
| `*.xlsx` (ADO tabular export) | `excel` | `testcases/**/*.xlsx` |

## ID writeback format per framework

After the first push, ado-sync writes the Azure Test Case ID back into each local file:

| local.type | ID written as |
|---|---|
| gherkin / markdown | `@tc:12345` tag above Scenario or ### heading |
| csharp (MSTest) | `[TestProperty("tc", "12345")]` above `[TestMethod]` |
| csharp (NUnit) | `[Property("tc", "12345")]` above `[Test]` |
| java (JUnit 4 / TestNG) | `// @tc:12345` comment above `@Test` |
| java (JUnit 5) | `@Tag("tc:12345")` above `@Test` |
| python | `@pytest.mark.tc(12345)` above `def test_*` |
| javascript / detox | `// @tc:12345` comment above `it()` or `test()` |
| playwright | `annotation: { type: 'tc', description: '12345' }` in test options |
| cypress | `// @tc:12345` comment above `it()` |
| espresso / xcuitest / flutter | `// @tc:12345` comment above test method |
| robot | `tc:12345` in `[Tags]` row |
| csv | numeric ID in column A |
| excel | numeric ID in cell A1 |

## Auth options

```json
{ "auth": { "type": "pat",             "token": "$AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN" } }
{ "auth": { "type": "accessToken",     "token": "$SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN" } }
{ "auth": { "type": "managedIdentity"  } }
```

PAT required scopes: **Test Management (Read & Write)**, Work Items (Read)

## Key CLI flags

```
ado-sync push --dry-run                        preview only
ado-sync push --tags "@smoke and not @wip"     filter by tag
ado-sync push --config-override testPlan.id=99 override config at runtime
ado-sync push --ai-provider heuristic          AI test step generation (no model needed)
ado-sync push --ai-provider openai --ai-key $OPENAI_API_KEY
ado-sync push --ai-provider anthropic --ai-key $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
ado-sync status --output json                  machine-readable output
ado-sync publish-test-results --testPlan 32953 --testSuite 32954   planned run with TC linkage
ado-sync publish-test-results --testConfiguration "Windows 10"     set test configuration
```

> **TC linkage requires planned runs.** Azure DevOps silently ignores `testCase.id` on
> unplanned runs. Use `--testPlan` and `--testSuite` so results are linked to Test Cases
> through test points.

## Suite routing

Automatically route Test Cases into named suites by tag:

```json
"testPlan": {
  "id": 1234,
  "suiteRouting": [
    { "tags": "@smoke", "suite": "Smoke" },
    { "tags": "@regression", "suite": "Regression" },
    { "suite": "General" }
  ]
}
```

## Conflict handling

```json
"sync": { "conflictAction": "overwrite" }   // local wins (default)
"sync": { "conflictAction": "skip" }        // keep Azure version
"sync": { "conflictAction": "fail" }        // abort push, list conflicts
```

## Environment variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN` | PAT or access token |
| `ADO_SYNC_CONFIG` | Path to config file (used by MCP server) |
| `DEBUG=1` | Show full stack traces on errors |

A `.env` file in the working directory is loaded automatically.

## VS Code Extension

`ado-sync-vscode` — VS Code extension (marketplace ID: `PavanMudigonda.ado-sync-vscode`)

Requires `ado-sync` npm package installed globally and an `ado-sync.json` config in the workspace root.

Features:
- CodeLens: inline View / Push / Fetch links above every `@tc:12345` tag
- Hover tooltips: hover `@tc:` to open Test Case in Azure DevOps
- Sidebar tree: Testing panel lists spec files and linked test cases with line numbers
- Status bar: live sync state, click to run status

Commands (Command Palette):
`ado-sync: Push`, `ado-sync: Push (Dry Run)`, `ado-sync: Pull`, `ado-sync: Pull (Dry Run)`,
`ado-sync: Status`, `ado-sync: Validate Config`, `ado-sync: Generate Spec from Story`,
`ado-sync: Fetch Test Case`, `ado-sync: Publish Test Results`

Settings:
- `ado-sync.configPath` (string, default "") — config file path relative to workspace root
- `ado-sync.showCodeLens` (boolean, default true) — toggle CodeLens
- `ado-sync.autoStatus` (boolean, default false) — auto-run status on spec file save
- `ado-sync.outputLevel` (string, default "normal") — "normal" | "verbose" | "quiet"

## MCP server

`ado-sync-mcp` starts an MCP stdio server exposing 13 tools:
`validate_config`, `get_test_cases`, `get_test_case`, `push_specs`, `pull_specs`,
`status`, `diff`, `generate_specs`, `get_work_items`, `publish_test_results`,
`create_issue`, `get_story_context`, `generate_manifest`

Register in Claude Code (`~/.claude/claude_desktop_config.json`):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "ado-sync": {
      "command": "ado-sync-mcp",
      "env": { "AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN": "<token>", "ADO_SYNC_CONFIG": "/path/to/ado-sync.json" }
    }
  }
}
```

## Documentation index

- docs/cli.md               — full CLI reference (all commands, flags, options)
- docs/configuration.md     — complete config schema reference
- docs/workflows.md         — framework-specific end-to-end examples
- docs/spec-formats.md      — spec file format details per framework
- docs/work-item-links.md   — linking Test Cases to User Stories / Bugs
- docs/publish-test-results.md — TRX / JUnit / Playwright result publishing
- docs/advanced.md          — suite routing, fieldUpdates, attachments, CI mode
- docs/mcp-server.md        — MCP server setup and tool reference
- docs/troubleshooting.md   — common errors and fixes
- docs/agent-setup.md       — step-by-step guide for AI agents doing autonomous setup
- docs/vscode-extension.md  — VS Code extension commands, settings, CodeLens, sidebar, troubleshooting
